body:
Hey, just a heads up — you mentioned last Sunday that you were going to start sending me that weekly work summary so I can keep track of what's going on with the Finthesiss projects. I know you're slammed but it would really help me feel less out of the loop when you come home stressed and I have no context.

Even just a few bullet points would be great. Did you set up a doc for it or are you still planning to?

— Marcus
snippet: Hey, just a heads up — you mentioned last Sunday that you were going to start se...
subject: Weekly status doc reminder

body:
Ugh, I completely forgot. I did start a Google Doc but never actually shared it with you. Sending you the link now — it's still pretty sparse but I'll fill it in properly this weekend.

The short version this week: Q3 roadmap review went okay, we pushed the onboarding flow to next sprint, and I have a 1:1 with the CTO on Friday that I'm mildly dreading. I'll add more detail to the doc tonight.

Sorry for dropping the ball on this. — Ryan
snippet: Ugh, I completely forgot. I did start a Google Doc but never actually shared it ...
subject: Re: Weekly status doc reminder

body:
Hey, are you free for lunch today? I was thinking we could try that new Thai place on Clement Street everyone's been talking about. Around noon or 12:30?

Let me know if today doesn't work and we can do tomorrow instead.
snippet: Hey, are you free for lunch today? I was thinking we could try that new Thai pla...
subject: Lunch today?

body:
Yes! I've been wanting to try that place. 12:30 works better for me, I have a call that runs until 12:15.

Should I meet you there or do you want to walk over together? I can swing by the house if you're working from home today.
snippet: Yes! I've been wanting to try that place. 12:30 works better for me, I have a ca...
subject: Re: Lunch today?

body:
I'm actually already downtown running errands, so just meet me there. I'll grab us a table if I get there first.

See you at 12:30!
snippet: I'm actually already downtown running errands, so just meet me there. I'll grab ...
subject: Re: Lunch today?

body:
Hey, so I finally got the updated floor plan PDFs from the contractor. There are three layout options and honestly I can't decide between option B and option C. Option B keeps the island where it is but opens up the pantry wall, and option C moves the island closer to the window but loses some cabinet space. Can you take a look tonight? I want to get back to them by Thursday.

Also, the tile samples are still on the kitchen counter if you want to compare them against the renders.
snippet: Hey, so I finally got the updated floor plan PDFs from the contractor. There are...
subject: Design review for the kitchen renovation

body:
I looked at the PDFs on my lunch break. I'm leaning toward option C — natural light in the kitchen is way more important to me than the extra cabinet space, and we can always add a small shelving unit near the back door to compensate.

That said, the pantry wall opening in option B is really nice. Do you know if we can ask them to combine elements from both? Like option C's island placement but with the pantry change from option B? Might be worth a quick call before you commit.
snippet: I looked at the PDFs on my lunch break. I'm leaning toward option C — natural li...
subject: Re: Design review for the kitchen renovation

body:
I actually asked them about that exact combo this afternoon! The contractor said it's doable but would add about $800 to the estimate because of how the plumbing runs near the pantry wall. So it depends on whether we think it's worth it.

I ran the numbers and we're still under budget even with the extra cost, so I'm okay with it if you are. Let me know and I'll confirm with them tonight. They need a decision by end of day Thursday at the latest.
snippet: I actually asked them about that exact combo this afternoon! The contractor said...
subject: Re: Design review for the kitchen renovation

body:
Yes, let's do the combo. $800 is worth it to get exactly what we want — we're going to live with this kitchen for years. Go ahead and confirm with them tonight.

One last thing: can you ask them to send a revised render once they update the plan? I want to see it before they actually start any demo work. Thanks for handling all of this, by the way. You're doing most of the heavy lifting on this project.

— Ryan
snippet: Yes, let's do the combo. $800 is worth it to get exactly what we want — we're go...
subject: Re: Design review for the kitchen renovation

body:
Hey, I finally looked into the Portland weekend. The Ace Hotel has availability June 27-29 and it's within budget. Should I go ahead and book two nights, or were you thinking we'd leave Sunday morning instead of Monday?

Also, are we driving or flying? I checked and flights are around $180 round trip each if we book by end of week. Driving is obviously cheaper but it's a 6-hour haul each way.

Let me know what you're thinking and I'll get moving on it.
snippet: Hey, I finally looked into the Portland weekend. The Ace Hotel has availability ...
subject: Portland trip - dates and hotel

body:
Let's fly, I don't want to burn a whole day each way driving. Go ahead and book the Ace for two nights — Sunday morning checkout works fine for me, I don't have anything until Monday afternoon anyway.

Can you check if the flights are direct or if there's a layover? I'd pay a little more for nonstop if there's an option. Also, Jenna mentioned she might want to join us Friday night for dinner if we get in early enough. I told her I'd confirm once we had flights locked in.
snippet: Let's fly, I don't want to burn a whole day each way driving. Go ahead and book ...
subject: Re: Portland trip - dates and hotel

body:
Booked the Ace — confirmation is in your email. The nonstop on Alaska is $210 each, departs 11:40am and lands at 1:15pm, so we'd have plenty of time to get settled before dinner. Layover option saves $60 total and adds two hours, so I say skip it.

I went ahead and grabbed the nonstop tickets, hope that's okay. I'll Venmo request you your half.

Told Jenna we'd meet her at 7 at that Italian place she likes on NW 21st. She seemed excited.
snippet: Booked the Ace — confirmation is in your email. The nonstop on Alaska is $210 ea...
subject: Re: Portland trip - dates and hotel

body:
Perfect, nonstop was the right call. Venmo request received, I'll send it tonight.

One thing — can you make sure the hotel room has two keys ready? Last time they only gave us one at check-in and it was annoying when we split up for a few hours. Also worth confirming they have parking since I think Jenna might need to drive over from her sister's place.

I'm really looking forward to this. It's been way too long since we've had a proper trip.
snippet: Perfect, nonstop was the right call. Venmo request received, I'll send it tonigh...
subject: Re: Portland trip - dates and hotel

body:
Called the hotel and they noted the two-key request on the reservation. They said street parking near the hotel is pretty easy on weekends so Jenna should be fine, but I sent her the address of a nearby garage just in case.

I think we're all set. I'll pack light so we can just do carry-on. Reminder to charge your camera — you always forget and then complain about using your phone the whole trip.

See you at the airport June 27th!
snippet: Called the hotel and they noted the two-key request on the reservation. They sai...
subject: Re: Portland trip - dates and hotel

body:
Hey, just got an email from the home security company saying our annual plan is up for renewal next week. It's $180 for another year. I know we talked about switching to a different provider but I can't remember if we made a decision on that.

Can you take a look and let me know? I don't want it to auto-renew if we're planning to cancel, but I also don't want to accidentally lose coverage if we're keeping it.

Marc
snippet: Hey, just got an email from the home security company saying our annual plan is ...
subject: Service renewal - did you want to keep it?

body:
Yeah let's just renew it for now. I looked into the other company a few weeks ago and their pricing wasn't that much better once you factor in the equipment fees. $180 for another year of the current plan is fine.

Go ahead and approve the renewal if you get the prompt. I'll set a reminder for next spring so we have more time to compare options before it comes up again.

Ryan
snippet: Yeah let's just renew it for now. I looked into the other company a few weeks ag...
subject: Re: Service renewal - did you want to keep it?

body:
Hey, saw your Slack message last night about the auth migration. Can you walk me through the cutover plan before you loop in the rest of the team? I want to make sure we're aligned on timing before you send the all-hands email.

Specifically I'm wondering about the rollback window. Are we planning a hard cutover or are we running both systems in parallel for a few days? Last time we did something like this the session invalidation caused a bunch of support tickets.

— Marcus
snippet: Hey, saw your Slack message last night about the auth migration. Can you walk me...
subject: auth cutover plan

body:
Good timing, I was about to draft the team email. The plan is a phased rollout — new auth stack goes live for internal users Friday night, then we flip the flag for external accounts the following Tuesday after we've had a full weekend to soak.

We're keeping the legacy token validation layer running in read-only mode for 72 hours post-cutover so we can reissue sessions without forcing everyone to log out. That should prevent the support ticket flood.

Can you do a final review of the runbook today? I want sign-off from you before I send anything to the broader group.

— Ryan
snippet: Good timing, I was about to draft the team email. The plan is a phased rollout —...
subject: Re: auth cutover plan

body:
Phased rollout sounds right to me. The 72-hour parallel window is smart — I'd just make sure the on-call rotation is fully staffed through Wednesday in case something surfaces late.

I'll go through the runbook this afternoon and leave comments in the doc. As long as the rollback steps are clearly sequenced I'm comfortable giving the green light.

Go ahead and send the team email once I've signed off. Should have notes back to you by 3.

— Marcus
snippet: Phased rollout sounds right to me. The 72-hour parallel window is smart — I'd ju...
subject: Re: auth cutover plan
